author: y4rr
content:
Spun out of #598. That issue landed the classification block (precision / recall / f1 / roc_auc / log_loss / confusion_matrix / hamming_loss / balanced_accuracy / matthews_corrcoef / cohen_kappa). The ranking + clustering surface is the remaining tail.

## Ranking (\`ferrolearn_metrics::ranking\`)
- \`dcg_score\` / \`ndcg_score\` — graded-relevance ranking metrics (Array2<f64> for true relevance, Array2<F> for predicted scores)
- \`coverage_error\` — minimum number of top-ranked labels needed to cover all true labels
- \`label_ranking_average_precision_score\`
- \`label_ranking_loss\`

## Clustering (\`ferrolearn_metrics::clustering\`)
- \`silhouette_score\` — needs Array2<F> samples + Array1<isize> labels
- \`silhouette_samples\` — per-sample silhouette
- \`adjusted_rand_score\` — pair-counting agreement
- \`adjusted_mutual_info\` — info-theoretic agreement
- \`davies_bouldin_score\` — cluster separation

## Adapter additions needed
The ranking/clustering metrics use \`Array2\` and \`Array1<isize>\` — the ml adapter currently only has \`tensor_to_array1<T>\` / \`tensor_to_array2<T>\` / \`tensor_to_array1_usize\`. We'd add:
- \`tensor_to_array1_isize\` (for cluster labels — sklearn uses signed because \`-1\` is the noise label in DBSCAN)
- \`tensor_to_array2_f64\` (for predicted-score matrices)
- \`tensor_to_array2_usize\` (for graded-relevance ground-truth)

These are small; the heavy lift is just the wrapper count.

## Notes
- All these metrics live in \`ferrolearn-metrics 0.3.0\` already — the wrappers are pure dispatch.
- Less commonly used than the classification block, hence \`low\` priority.
